assessment.case_summary: F1–F4 establish a EUR 18,000 annual subscription, an executed DPA, intended processing of customer-related material, and several documented controls. They also establish that no customer-specific EU/EEA-only commitment exists and that the SOC 2 Type II examination remains in progress. F5, described as the later supplemental residency assurance, was not supplied. The evidence record therefore contains a blocking residency gap, outstanding internal approval routing, a security-assurance gap, and a material conflict concerning advance notice of new subprocessors. No final vendor decision is made.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Yes. Customers receive at least 30 days' advance notice before a material new subprocessor is authorized to process Customer Data.
This section does not promise the 30 days' advance notice stated in questionnaire response SUB-02. The executed DPA controls unless the parties sign a specific amendment.

Conflicting answers across a proposal, questionnaire, security page, contract, or letter require human review. The conflict must remain visible even if a later document resolves the operational question.

conflicts.summary: Questionnaire response SUB-02 promises at least 30 days' advance notice before a material new subprocessor is authorized, but executed DPA section 9.6 permits authorization and non-EEA processing without prior notice. The DPA expressly acknowledges the discrepancy and controls contractually, while policy requires the conflict to remain visible and receive human review.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Annual subscription | EUR 18,000 excluding VAT

facts.statement: The written proposal states an annual subscription price of EUR 18,000 excluding VAT.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: As of this DPA's Effective Date, the parties have not recorded an EU-only location commitment for the Caldera Works Europe tenant.
Unless a customer-specific term expressly includes them, a primary-region setting may not restrict all backups, telemetry, security logs, support access, email, or account-administration data.
This commercial proposal does not itself create an EU-only data-residency commitment.

facts.statement: The supplied evidence establishes that written EU/EEA-only residency confirmation covering customer content, backups, and core processing is not currently in place.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Controller signature status | Executed — fictional fixture
Processor signature status | Executed — fictional fixture

facts.statement: DPA version 4.3 was executed by both fictional parties with an effective date of 2026-07-12.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Is a SOC 2 Type II report currently issued? | In progress. | Readiness summary only | Missing issued report
This DPA does not convert an unfinished examination into an issued report.

facts.statement: No issued SOC 2 Type II or documented equivalent assurance report is established by the supplied evidence; the SOC 2 examination is in progress.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Initial term | 36 months

facts.statement: The proposed initial subscription term is 36 months.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Annual subscription | EUR 18,000 excluding VAT
Initial term | 36 months

facts.statement: The base subscription commitment over the 36-month initial term is EUR 54,000 excluding VAT and any additional usage, calculated from the stated annual subscription and initial term.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Above EUR 15,000 | Director approval in addition to Budget owner and Operations
The annual subscription exceeds EUR 15,000.

facts.statement: The annual price exceeds EUR 15,000, so policy requires Director approval in addition to Budget owner and Operations approval.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Customer users may submit internal documents and limited customer-related material when performing approved business tasks.
Customer personnel, business contacts, customer contacts, prospective customers, suppliers, and individuals mentioned in documents submitted by Authorized Users.

facts.statement: The proposed use permits users to submit internal documents and limited customer-related material, and the DPA processing scope includes customer contacts and customer-service content.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Nerythica will not use Customer Content from an enterprise tenant to train a shared foundation model unless Customer gives separate express written authorization.

facts.statement: The executed DPA prohibits use of enterprise Customer Content to train a shared foundation model unless the customer gives separate express written authorization.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Nerythica may authorize a new or replacement Subprocessor to Process Customer Data, and may permit that Processing to occur outside the EEA, without providing Customer prior notice

facts.statement: The executed DPA does not require prior notice before a new or replacement subprocessor begins processing Customer Data.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Encrypted backups use rolling retention and ordinarily expire within 90 days after deletion from active systems.
Residual backup copies expire through ordinary rotation, normally within 90 additional days.

facts.statement: Encrypted backups ordinarily expire within 90 days after deletion from active systems, and termination-related residual backups normally expire within 90 additional days after active-system deletion.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Customer must use qualified human review appropriate to the use case.
Customer is responsible for appropriate human review of model output and for final business decisions.

facts.statement: The executed DPA requires qualified human review of model output and leaves final business decisions with the customer.

missing_evidence.description: F5 was not supplied. The current documents expressly state that no customer-specific EU/EEA-only commitment exists. Because Customer Data is in scope, policy treats the missing written residency confirmation as a blocking control.
missing_evidence.requested_evidence: Supply the signed customer-specific F5 assurance or an executed amendment confirming that Customer Content, backups, and core processing for the identified tenant will be stored and processed only in the EU/EEA, with explicit coverage of inference, restoration, support access, telemetry, and subprocessors.

missing_evidence.description: The SOC 2 Type II examination is only in progress, ISO/IEC 27001 certification is absent, and no issued documented equivalent was supplied.
missing_evidence.requested_evidence: Provide a current issued SOC 2 Type II report, ISO/IEC 27001 certificate, or documented equivalent assurance, including issue date, scope, period covered, exceptions, and any required bridge documentation.

missing_evidence.description: No recorded Budget owner, Operations, or Director approval was supplied. The EUR 18,000 annual recurring price requires all three approval roles.
missing_evidence.requested_evidence: Provide the recorded approvals, approver identities and roles, conditions, timestamps, and final authorized human decision required by policy.

missing_evidence.description: No named internal service owner was supplied, although policy requires one before Customer Data is uploaded.
missing_evidence.requested_evidence: Identify and record the accountable internal service owner, including responsibility for configuration, access, retention, output review, vendor monitoring, and offboarding.

missing_evidence.description: The evidence permits support access from approved locations and non-EEA processing, but does not identify all support-access countries applicable to this tenant.
missing_evidence.requested_evidence: Provide a dated list of support-access countries, relevant entities and subprocessors, access purposes, transfer mechanisms, and whether the requested EU/EEA-only assurance restricts such access.

missing_evidence.description: F1 is expressly non-binding and not an order form. No executed enterprise order form containing the final price, regional configuration, term, and incorporated documents was supplied.
missing_evidence.requested_evidence: Provide the final executed order form and applicable Master Subscription Agreement, and verify that their commercial, location, precedence, renewal, and usage terms match the reviewed record.

benefits: Preserves the mandatory residency and approval controls.
Allows F5 and the final contractual documents to be evaluated before commitment.
Keeps the subprocessor-notice conflict visible for legal and procurement review.
options.description: Keep the case open without approving, rejecting, activating, or uploading Customer Data while the blocking residency evidence, approval routing, and human conflict review are completed.
options.label: Hold the approval decision pending required evidence
preconditions: Do not upload Customer Data or activate production processing.
Request F5 and all other identified missing evidence.
Route the conflict and non-EEA transfer clauses to human legal review.
risks: Delays deployment and may require commercial validity or pricing to be reconfirmed.
The vendor may not provide an assurance broad enough to cover backups, core processing, support, telemetry, and subprocessors.

benefits: Allows limited usability and administrative-control testing while evidence is collected.
Avoids invoking the Customer Data residency and executed-DPA upload conditions for the test dataset.
options.description: Permit only a separately authorized technical evaluation using synthetic or public test data, with no Customer Data, no production activation, and no representation that vendor approval has been granted.
options.label: Conduct a restricted non-customer-data evaluation
preconditions: Obtain explicit human authorization for the evaluation.
Use only synthetic or approved public data.
Disable integrations and workflows that could ingest company or customer documents.
Apply access restrictions, user instructions, monitoring, and a fixed end date.
Do not sign or activate a production order through this option.
risks: Test controls may fail to prevent accidental entry of Customer Data.
A synthetic-data evaluation may not demonstrate production performance or compliance.
Commercial or contractual commitments could be created if the evaluation is not separately controlled.

benefits: Provides a route to evaluate the vendor against complete residency, security, commercial, and contractual evidence.
Allows conditions and ownership responsibilities to be recorded by authorized employees.
options.description: Once blocking evidence is supplied, prepare a refreshed evidence record for the Budget owner, Operations, Director, legal reviewer, and other authorized reviewers without treating remediation as automatic approval.
options.label: Resume the full human approval workflow after remediation
preconditions: Validate F5 against DPA sections 12 and 14 and the final order form.
Obtain the required security assurance or document the resulting policy treatment.
Resolve or formally review the subprocessor-notice conflict without hiding it.
Record a named internal service owner.
Complete Budget owner, Operations, and Director routing.
Record the final decision only through an authorized human approver.
risks: F5 may conflict with the DPA or have insufficient scope.
The issued assurance report may contain qualifications or scope exclusions.
Human reviewers may determine that residual transfer, subprocessor, security, or commercial risks are unacceptable.

conditions: Maintain the residency item as blocking until compliant written evidence is validated.
Do not upload Customer Data or activate production service.
Do not treat the executed DPA, vendor questionnaire, or this assessment as final vendor approval.
Require an authorized employee to make and record the final decision.
recommendation.rationale: Option 001 is the appropriate procedural next step because F5 is absent, the current contract affirmatively lacks the mandatory EU/EEA-only commitment, required internal approvals are not evidenced, and a material contractual conflict requires human review. This recommendation preserves controls and does not constitute approval or rejection of the vendor.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: The Service uses global cloud infrastructure and may Process Customer Data in the United States, European Union, and other locations
If items 1 or 2 are missing, the case has a blocking control and cannot be approved.

risks.summary: The current contract permits processing in the United States and other locations, support access from approved locations, and subprocessor processing outside the EEA. This does not satisfy the mandatory EU/EEA-only control for Customer Data.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Do you hold ISO/IEC 27001 certification? | No.
In progress. | Readiness summary only | Missing issued report

risks.summary: The vendor describes a security programme and annual testing, but no issued SOC 2 Type II, ISO/IEC 27001 certificate, or documented equivalent assurance report was supplied.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Customer may object on reasonable data-protection grounds by written notice received within ten calendar days after publication of the updated list.
Customers receive at least 30 days' advance notice before a material new subprocessor is authorized

risks.summary: The executed DPA permits new subprocessors and non-EEA processing without prior notice, contrary to the questionnaire's 30-day advance-notice statement. The customer may learn of changes only after publication and then has ten days to object.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Initial term | 36 months
Renewal | Automatic 12-month renewal unless either party gives 60 days' notice
Price review | Up to 5% at each renewal

risks.summary: The proposal creates a base 36-month subscription exposure of EUR 54,000 excluding VAT, additional usage charges, automatic 12-month renewal, and potential renewal price increases of up to 5%.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Model output may be incomplete, inconsistent, or incorrect.
The service is intended for approximately 80 employees across operations, legal, sales, and customer-success teams.

risks.summary: The service may produce incomplete, inconsistent, or incorrect output. Use across legal, sales, operations, and customer-success workflows creates reliance risk unless qualified human review and use-case restrictions are implemented.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: This proposal is non-binding until an authorized order form is signed.
Status: Vendor proposal supplied for review; not an order form.

risks.summary: The commercial proposal is non-binding and the final order form was not supplied, so final configuration, pricing, regional terms, and incorporated contractual obligations cannot yet be verified against the assessment.
